data.title: Eve Air Mobility gets $150M debt for aircraft development
data.description:
• Eve Air Mobility (EVEX) secured $150M in debt financing to boost its electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft development.
  • This funding comes from a syndicate including Itaú, Banco do Brasil, Citibank, and Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group as a five-year loan.
  • The proceeds will support Eve's research and development, including the integration of its eVTOL aircraft into a comprehensive urban air mobility ecosystem. This funding accelerates technological progress and strengthens partnerships with infrastructure providers and regulatory bodies. 
  • This brings Eve's total funding to $1.2B and aims for commercialization through 2028.
  • The company recently completed the first flight of its full-scale engineering prototype at Embraer's test facility in Brazil.

data.title: Digital Currency X Technology announces 12 for 1 share consolidation
908 Devices shares jump on preliminary Q4 and FY25 financial update
data.description:
• 908 Devices (MASS) announced preliminary fourth-quarter revenue of approximately $17.2 million, exceeding consensus expectations of $15.92 million and representing 20% growth from $14.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2024. The outperformance was driven by continued strength in the U.S. state and local channel, accelerating international adoption of VipIR, and increased sales to federal and defense customers.

  • The company also achieved its goal of positive adjusted EBITDA in the fourth quarter.

  • For fiscal year 2025, revenue is expected to be approximately $56.0 million, an increase of 17% from $47.7 million last year. This result aligns with the high end of management’s guidance and comes in slightly above consensus estimates of $54.77 million.

  • 908 Devices plans to report its full fourth-quarter and fiscal year 2025 financial results later this quarter, at which time it will also provide its outlook for 2026.

  • MASS +5.8% premarket to $5.79
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ImmunityBio extends gains after FDA views on Anktiva label expansion
data.description:
Following a more than two-fold rise in value last week, shares of ImmunityBio (IBRX) added ~16% in the premarket on Tuesday after the company announced updates from a meeting held with the FDA regarding its plans to seek a label expansion for its bladder cancer therapy, Anktiva.

The Type B End-of-Phase meeting focused on the company’s planned supplemental Biologics License Application ((sBLA)) for Anktiva in combination with Bacillus Calmette-Guérin ((BCG)) for BCG-unresponsive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer with papillary tumors.

The immunotherapy is already available in the U.S. with BCG for NMIBC patients with or without papillary tumors who are unresponsive to BCG. In May 2025, the FDA issued a Refusal to File ((RTF)) letter regarding ImmunityBio’s (IBRX) sBLA aimed at expanding Anktiva to NMIBC with papillary tumors.

The San Diego, California-based biotech noted that during the meeting, the FDA recommended the company submit additional data to support a resubmission of sBLA. ImmunityBio (IBRX) indicated a 30-day timeline to provide additional data, stating that it will not need to conduct any further clinical studies.

data.title: Light AI gets new executive chief
data.description:
• Light AI (OHCFF), a healthcare technology company specializing in AI-driven diagnostic and wellness solutions, named John R. Luna as CEO, effective January 19, 2026.
  • The company's former CEO and founder, Peter Whitehead, is transitioning the role of chief innovation officer to continue to lead the company's medical diagnostic offering and related intellectual property expansion.
  • Most recently, Mr. Luna served as Executive Advisor to EssilorLuxottica, where he collaborated with the Super Audio team on advanced smart eyewear and software-based hearing technologies.

data.title: National Healthcare Properties announces draft registration statement for public stock offering
data.description:
National Healthcare Properties (HLTC) on Tuesday announced the confidential submission of a draft registration statement on Form S-11 of the Securities and Exchange Commission in connection with the proposed public offering and initial listing of shares of common stock.

The number of shares to be sold and the price range for the proposed offering have not yet been determined.

Net proceeds are expected to be used to repay indebtedness outstanding under the company's revolving credit facility, to fund external growth with potential future property acquisitions, and for other general corporate purposes.

data.title: Netflix makes a revised all-cash offer for Warner Bros. Discovery
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) updated its offer for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) on Tuesday. In an SEC filing, the streaming giant said it would make an all-cash offer for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) at $27.75 per share. 

Warner Bros. (WBD) said it will hold a special meeting of shareholders to vote on the deal before April. The company highlighted that the all-cash transaction provides enhanced certainty around the value WBD stockholders will receive at closing, eliminating market-based variability.

"Today’s revised merger agreement brings us even closer to combining two of the greatest storytelling companies in the world, and with it even more people will enjoy the entertainment they love to watch the most," stated Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) CEO David Zaslav. "By coming together with Netflix, we will combine the stories Warner Bros. has told that have captured the world’s attention for more than a century and ensure audiences continue to enjoy them for generations to come," he added.

Netflix (NFLX) co-CEO  Ted Sarandos highlighted that the revised all-cash agreement will enable an expedited timeline to a stockholder vote and provide greater financial certainty at $27.75 per share in cash, plus the value from the planned separation of Discovery Global. "The acquisition will also significantly expand U.S. production capacity and investment in original programming, driving job creation and long-term industry growth," he maintained.

Netflix's (NFLX) first formal offer for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) studios and streaming assets emerged in the initial bidding round on November 20, when it submitted a non-binding proposal valuing the assets at about $27.00 per share.

Shares of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) slipped 0.5% to $28.44 in premarket action. Netflix (NFLX) edged 0.7% higher in the early session. Paramount Skydance (PSKY) was down 0.7%.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: D.R. Horton tops FQ1 estimates; reaffirms FY26 outlook
Otis names Enrique Miñarro Viseras as operations chief
data.description:
• Otis Worldwide (OTIS) appointed Enrique Miñarro Viseras as COO, effective January 16, 2026, following a Board decision on January 13, 2026.
  • Miñarro Viseras previously led Otis EMEA since October 2023 and served as President of Otis EMEA & Latin America from May to December 2025.

data.title: The Cass Transportation Index weakens in December amid winter storms
data.description:
The December 2025 Cass Transportation Index report indicated more softness in the freight market, with volume lower but stabilizing amid firming pricing. The shipments component of the Cass Freight Index fell 7.2% month over month in December, or 3.2% on a seasonally adjusted basis, hitting a new cycle low and leaving volume down 7.5% year over year versus a 6.1% decline for full-year 2025. Weather was noted to be a key headwind for the freight transportation industry, as three major winter storms in the Midwest disrupted the highway network and created pent-up demand that was still visible in the spot market in early January, while holiday spending outpaced freight flows, implying retail inventory destocking across modes.

The expenditures component of the Cass Index declined 1.9% month over month in December and was down 0.6% year over year for the month after a 1.2% year-over-year dip in November. Despite the modest annual decline of 0.5% for 2025, expenditures remain elevated after surging 38% in 2021 and 23% in 2022 before falling 19% in 2023 and 11% in 2024, implying higher freight costs as spending has not fallen as much as shipments.

The Cass Truckload Linehaul Index rose 1.0% month over month in December after edging up 0.1% in November and posted a 1.8% increase for 2025 following declines of 10% in 2023 and 3.4% in 2024. For-hire volumes were soft in the fourth quarter due to storms, payback from pre-tariff shipping, and excess private-fleet capacity, but an active winter is removing some capacity and has driven the strongest four-week run in spot rates in four years.

Looking ahead, the update from Cass Information Systems indicates that following significant Q4 destocking, a favorable Supreme Court decision on IEEPA tariffs could become a positive catalyst for freight demand.

The Cass report is significant for many transportation stocks, including Knight-Swift (KNX), Werner (WERN), J.B. Hunt (JBHT), Schneider (SNDR), Old Dominion (ODFL), Saia (SAIA), XPO (XPO), C.H. Robinson (CHRW), RXO (RXO), TFI International (TFII), Hub Group (HUBG), Landstar (LSTR), and even large parcel carriers UPS (UPS) and FedEx (FDX).
